eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Mayor Mark Sutcliffe leads Polymarket odds at 65% implied probability for the October 26, 2026 Ottawa municipal election, reflecting trader consensus on his re-election advantages including name recognition, established campaign infrastructure, and a 2022 victory amid split opposition. A Liaison Strategies poll released April 28 (conducted April 11-12) shows him ahead 46%-37% over Councillor Jeff Leiper among decided voters, but with roughly half undecided—a group that historically favors incumbents—despite sinking approvals on transit (70% disapprove), Lansdowne 2.0 redevelopment (62% view spending as inappropriate), housing affordability, and traffic. Fragmented challengers like homebuilder Alex Lawson (8.1%, eroding Sutcliffe's west-end base) and others prevent anti-incumbent consolidation, positioning Leiper (22%) as the primary alternative while Catherine McKenney (3.3%) trails post-provincial MPP win. Nominations open soon, with high undecideds signaling race fluidity.

markets.question: Will Arouca qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Conference League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Conference League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Conference League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or the Primeira Liga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Spread: 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 2 at 9:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "1. FC Heidenheim 1846" if 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Bayern München vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 2 at 9:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich hold a commanding 12-point lead atop the Bundesliga table after 29 matches, with Heidenheim mired in the relegation zone on 19 points from four wins, facing a must-win survival battle away at the Allianz Arena. Bayern's dominance is underscored by recent 4-0 head-to-head routs and superior home form, though trader consensus reflects caution amid a mounting injury list—Serge Gnabry sidelined long-term by an adductor tear, joined by Lennart Karl (hamstring) and others—thinning attacking depth during a congested schedule featuring Champions League semis against PSG. Heidenheim contend without key forwards Sirlord Conteh (knee) and Mikkel Kaufmann (calf), amplifying Bayern's stylistic edge despite rotation risks.

markets.question: Will ForecastEx self-certify sports event contracts by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: CFTC's March 2026 staff advisory provides the clearest guidance yet for designated contract markets (DCMs) to self-certify sports event contracts, urging pre-filing engagement with staff and leagues like MLB to address manipulation and integrity risks amid surging prediction market volumes. An early April Third Circuit ruling affirmed CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction, preempting state gambling laws and blocking enforcement against DCM-traded sports outcomes, significantly lowering barriers. While Kalshi self-certified golf and tennis contracts that month, no further major filings have emerged in the past 30 days, leaving trader focus on whether incumbents like CME or challengers including Aristotle, Railbird, and LedgerX act before the June 30 deadline, per public CFTC records. Ongoing Ninth Circuit arguments in Nevada add uncertainty.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 32°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward a 34°C high in Kuala Lumpur on May 1, reflecting Malaysian Meteorological Department forecasts of a 35°C maximum amid isolated afternoon thunderstorms, corroborated by AccuWeather's 94°F (34°C) projection and BBC's 35°C outlook. Recent April highs reached 36°C during a heat spell, but May's onset of southwest monsoon influences boosts diurnal convection, fostering clouds and showers that often cap peaks 1–3°C below clear-sky potentials via reduced insolation. Historical May averages hover at 32°C, with urban heat island effects in Kuala Lumpur adding variability; model runs diverge on storm timing, explaining tight clustering around 31–35°C. Watch MetMalaysia updates tomorrow for refined guidance as resolution nears.

events.title: Chicago Fire FC vs. FC Cincinnati
events.description: This event is for the upcoming MLS game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Chicago Fire FC and FC Cincinnati.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Fire FC holds trader consensus at 55% implied probability to win at home against FC Cincinnati, driven by their third-place Eastern Conference standing (17 points from nine matches) and strong recent form, including a dramatic 3-3 draw at TQL Stadium on April 18 where Hugo Cuypers scored twice. FC Cincinnati languishes around 10th (12 points from 10 games, -4 goal difference), hampered by a crippling defensive injury crisis with seven players out—including center backs Matt Miazga, Miles Robinson, and Teenage Hadebe, plus midfielder Obinna Nwobodo—exacerbating their league-worst away record with zero road wins. Chicago's healthier squad, bolstered by home advantage at Soldier Field, positions them as the slight favorite in this closely contested matchup, though Cincinnati's attacking threats like Evander keep the draw viable at 21.5%.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including AtlasIntel (April 22-27) showing Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 46.6% and Flávio Bolsonaro at 39.7% in first-round voting intentions, alongside BTG Pactual/Nexus (April 24-26) with Lula at 41% and Flávio at 36%, have solidified trader consensus that Flávio secures second place ahead of the October 4 first round. No other candidates, such as Romeu Zema or Ronaldo Caiado polling under 6%, pose a threat to this positioning, explaining Flávio's 66% implied probability. Lula's 19.5% reflects the market's assessment of his likely first-round lead, while fragmented fields for remaining contenders keep their odds low amid ongoing campaign consolidation.

Park's pledges to restart Daejeon integration and leverage regional strengths contrast Kim's Silicon Valley ambitions, boosting challenger appeal. Late-breaking scandals, conservative turnout surges, or unresolved administrative issues could challenge this positioning before election day.

markets.question: Exact Score: Wolverhampton Wanderers FC 1 - 0 Sunderland AFC?
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Wolverhampton Wanderers FC and Sunderland AFC, scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Wolverhampton Wanderers FC vs. Sunderland AFC match originally scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

events.title: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty at 97% against a Bill Clinton divorce by June 30, driven by the complete absence of recent credible reports, legal filings, public statements, or rumors indicating marital discord. The Clintons, married since 1975, have maintained public unity despite historical infidelity allegations during Bill Clinton's presidency, with no verified developments in the past 30 days altering this stability—recent social media mentions recycle decades-old anecdotes from advisors like Dick Morris without new evidence. At ages 79 and 78, structural factors like advanced age and long-term partnership further solidify expectations. Only extraordinary late-breaking events, such as a sudden health crisis or scandalous revelation, could shift odds in the remaining two months.

markets.question: Will A Just Russia – For Truth (SRZP) win the second-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) for second-most seats in the September 2026 State Duma election at 34.5%, closely trailed by New People (NL) at 31% and Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR) at 26.5%, reflecting a tight contest among systemic opposition parties behind dominant United Russia. Recent VCIOM polls through mid-April show NL surging to 13.4% in proportional list voting intentions, overtaking LDPR (10.1%) and matching KPRF (10.9%), fueled by party conventions and Kremlin-backed promotion as a middle-class alternative amid economic pressures eroding ER support. KPRF holds an edge via historical strength in single-member districts, but regional arrests of its activists and LDPR's nationalist appeals keep the race fragmented. Separation could arise from federal party list approvals, electronic primaries, or shifts in undecided voters (10-20%) ahead of the campaign launch.

markets.question: Will USA win Group D in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin Group D battle, with co-host USA and Türkiye deadlocked at 35% implied probabilities to top the standings, driven by USA's home-soil edges in all three matches—starting June 12 versus Paraguay at SoFi Stadium—and Türkiye's surging form from their late March playoff win over Kosovo, featuring stars like Hakan Çalhanoğlu, Kenan Yıldız, and Arda Güler. Recent friendlies underscore the parity: USA split results with Japan (0-1 loss away, 3-0 home win mid-April) after beating Paraguay and Australia 2-1 last year but falling 2-1 to Türkiye; Australia's FIFA Series triumphs (1-0 over Cameroon, 5-1 Curaçao end March) boost their physicality to 7.5%, while Paraguay's gritty CONMEBOL qualifiers and 1-2 friendly loss to Morocco position them third at 17% amid low-block threats and set-piece prowess. Head-to-head dynamics and no major injuries keep the race wide open.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 140-159 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Diego Dedura-Palomero vs. Matthew William Donald: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Diego Dedura-Palomero and Matthew William Donald in the Mauthausen, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger statistics.

events.title: Mauthausen: Diego Dedura-Palomero vs Matthew William Donald
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Diego Dedura-Palomero and Matthew William Donald in the Mauthausen, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Diego Dedura-Palomero' if Diego Dedura-Palomero advances against Matthew William Donald.

This market will resolve to 'Matthew William Donald' if Matthew William Donald advances against Diego Dedura-Palomero.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's closely contested March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, where Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement secured 29 seats to Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party's 28, Golob conceded on April 20 his inability to form a governing coalition, shifting momentum to the right-wing bloc. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined on April 25 to nominate a prime minister-designate amid deadlocked talks, directing the 90-seat National Assembly to propose a candidate needing 46 votes for an absolute majority within 14 days—or face a relative majority vote, followed potentially by snap elections. Trader consensus heavily favors Janša at 89%, driven by his SDS's pivotal role in coalition negotiations with smaller parties like NSi, positioning him as the leading candidate despite lingering uncertainty.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the top AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $260 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Trader sentiment reflects optimism around resilient iPhone sales defying a global smartphone slump, AI integration progress, and record recycled materials in products, tempered by recent leadership shifts announcing Tim Cook as executive chairman and John Ternus as CEO effective September 2026. Consensus anticipates steady revenue growth, with focus on services margins and guidance; post-earnings reaction could swing the stock toward key levels like $268 or $272, ahead of WWDC in June.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the South Carolina Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm South Carolina U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Senator Lindsey Graham's strong fundraising—over $11 million cash on hand—and endorsements from Donald Trump and Tim Scott solidify Republican dominance in South Carolina's deep-red Senate race, driving trader consensus to 79% for a GOP victory. Recent polls, including a March Impact Research survey showing Graham leading likely Democratic nominee Annie Andrews 47%-42%, reflect a competitive but structurally tilted contest amid the state's GOP supermajority and historical incumbent advantages. Paul Dans' April 10 withdrawal and endorsement of primary challenger Mark Lynch, where Graham leads 41%-21%, has prompted Inside Elections to shift the rating to Likely Republican on April 23. June 9 primaries loom as the next catalyst, with the general election November 3.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Obliterated" or "Obliteration" during King Charles visit?
markets.description:
King Charles is scheduled to visit the White House from April 27 to April 30, 2026 (https://time.com/article/2026/04/01/king-charles-camilla-royal-state-visit-washington-america-trump/). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring King Charles from April 27 through April 30, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and King Charles from April 27 through April 30, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

Vasilevskiy paced the league with 39 wins in 58 games, bolstered by a 2.31 goals-against average (second) and .912 save percentage (tied third), reflecting his heavy workload and elite consistency that propelled Tampa into playoff contention. markets.question: Will O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, O'Reilly Automotive is estimated to release earnings on April 29, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for O'Reilly Automotive's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.70 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if O'Reilly Automotive reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.70 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If O'Reilly Automotive releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Map Handicap: GH (-1.5) vs ODDIK Academy (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Game Hunters and ODDIK Academy in the CCT South America Series #1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 28 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Game Hunters" if Game Hunters wins 2 or more maps than ODDIK Academy in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "ODDIK Academy". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: GH (-1.5) vs ODDIK Academy (+1.5)

events.title: Counter-Strike: Game Hunters vs ODDIK Academy (BO3) - CCT South America Series #1 Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Game Hunters and ODDIK Academy in the CCT South America Series #1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 28 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Game Hunters" if Game Hunters win the match against ODDIK Academy.

This market will resolve to "ODDIK Academy" if ODDIK Academy win the match against Game Hunters.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: GameHunters heads into the CCT South America Series #1 group stage Swiss BO3 matchup against ODDIK Academy as slight favorites per trader consensus, buoyed by their HLTV world ranking of #134 and a 42.9% win rate over the last three months, including a 2-0 victory over ODDIK on April 2 in BetBoom Circuit X Mayhem. However, recent losses to Shinden (0-2 on April 28) and Imperial (1-2 on April 26) highlight defensive vulnerabilities on maps like Inferno (25% win rate) amid roster stability with starters abr, nyezin, bnc, and bsd. Unranked ODDIK Academy, inactive since late 2025 with mixed prior results, fields Ceruttera, PremiuM, jvZ, and max, posing upset potential in this low-tier South American qualifier where map vetoes and clutches could swing outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves hold a 3-2 series lead over the Nuggets in their NBA playoffs first-round matchup, with Game 6 set for Wednesday at Target Center in Minnesota. Anthony Edwards' left knee bone bruise from Game 4 sidelined him for Denver's 125-113 Game 5 comeback win, where Nikola Jokic posted a triple-double; his status remains uncertain ahead of the must-win for Minnesota, alongside Donte DiVincenzo's leg injury. Denver's Aaron Gordon is out with a significant left calf strain, impacting their frontcourt defense, while Peyton Watson nurses a hamstring issue. Minnesota's home-court edge and bench depth via Ayo Dosunmu's 43-point Game 4 explosion counter Nuggets' momentum and Jokic's dominance in a tightly contested series.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, held April 16 to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill, has solidified at approximately 20.7% after late-counted mail and provisional ballots from Democratic strongholds like Passaic County boosted her margin from an initial sub-20% at 95% reporting. This outcome in the safely blue suburban district—where Mejia outperformed Kamala Harris's 2024 presidential result by over 12 points—reflects strong progressive turnout against Republican Joe Hathaway, despite softer support in moderate towns like Livingston and Millburn with large Jewish populations wary of her left-wing Israel stance. Trader consensus prices a 20-25% victory at 91.5%, with final certification expected soon; rare shifts could arise from unresolved ballots or a recount, though no disputes have emerged.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's implied 64% probability as trader consensus favorite for the Roland Garros men's singles title stems from his breakthrough Monte-Carlo Masters victory two weeks ago—his first clay-court Masters 1000 crown, defeating Carlos Alcaraz in the final and Alexander Zverev in the semifinals—coupled with Alcaraz's withdrawal five days ago due to a right wrist injury that ends the two-time defending champion's three-peat bid. Sinner's world No. 1 status and 19-match winning streak underscore his adapted clay baseline power and improved sliding, elevating him above Zverev (9.5%), a perennial clay threat with strong surface history despite recent losses, and Djokovic (8.6%), relying on Grand Slam pedigree amid lighter 2026 clay prep. Medvedev and Khachanov trail at 4.3% on consistent but unproven deep runs.

Focus remains on routine Obama Foundation initiatives, including the Presidential Center's scheduled June 19 opening, amid 2026 midterm preparations where he earlier urged Democrats toward younger candidates, underscoring stability over disruption through year-end.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the PL party at 85.5% implied probability to secure the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, which renews 54 of 81 seats across states via majoritarian contests for one, two, or three positions per state depending on the cycle. This positioning stems from PL's deep bench of competitive candidates, bolstered by the party's 2022 congressional surge and sustained right-wing momentum amid tight presidential polls showing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) statistically tied with President Lula in recent Datafolha and Quaest surveys from early to mid-April. Late April state-level polls, including Vox Brasil in São Paulo (PL's Guilherme Derrite at 27%) and Quaest in Paraná (strong PL challengers), reinforce PL's edge in battleground races, outpacing PODEMOS, NOVO, and others despite uncertainties from candidate ineligibility rulings and coalition shifts.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 34% implied probability to win the 2026 WNBA championship after a stellar offseason, signing All-Star forward Satou Sabally to join a re-signed Big Three core of Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu, and Jonquel Jones for unmatched frontcourt depth and scoring versatility. Indiana Fever sit at 20.5% buoyed by Aliyah Boston's extension through 2029, Kelsey Mitchell's supermax re-signing, and defensive additions like Raven Johnson, building around Caitlin Clark's MVP-caliber play. Defending 2025 champions Las Vegas Aces hold 15% with A'ja Wilson's dominance and core retention including Jackie Young, though quieter moves temper expectations. Atlanta Dream's 12.5% reflects acquiring Angel Reese cheaply and locking in Rhyne Howard plus Allisha Gray long-term, while Golden State Valkyries' 12% stems from Gabby Williams' defensive boost in Year Two; Minnesota Lynx slipped to 11% amid key departures and Napheesa Collier's ankle surgery sidelining her until June, with Seattle Storm at 10.5% embracing a youth rebuild via top draft picks Awa Fam Thiam and Flau'jae Johnson post-expansion draft and free agency frenzies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman as the frontrunner at 51% implied probability to win the 2026 mayoral election, driven by anti-incumbent frustration over persistent homelessness and slow progress under Mayor Karen Bass despite voter-approved funding measures. Recent fundraising filings show Raman raising $530,000 and reality TV personality Spencer Pratt topping the field at $540,000 since January, outpacing Bass's cash reserves amid her 25% support in the latest UCLA Luskin poll, where 40% of likely primary voters remain undecided. Mixed polling—including a controversial March survey showing Raman leading—highlights volatility ahead of the June 2 nonpartisan primary, with top-two candidates advancing to a potential November runoff if no majority emerges.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Vegas Golden Knights' trader consensus at 60% implied probability to win the series reflects their home-ice advantage for Game 5 tonight at T-Mobile Arena and potential closeout Games 6-7, following Shea Theodore's overtime winner in a thrilling 5-4 Game 4 victory that evened the score at 2-2 after Utah Mammoth erased a 3-0 deficit. Vegas' playoff pedigree as Pacific Division champions and recent resilience—bolstered by Jack Eichel's three assists and Brett Howden's two goals—contrasts the Mammoth's gritty expansion debut, marked by Karel Vejmelka's strong .916 save percentage but hampered by Barrett Hayton's upper-body injury absence. High-event matchups and Vegas' depth in standings leaders like Mark Stone tilt sentiment despite Utah's Game 3 upset.

markets.question: Will Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 10, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Al Shabab at 34% and Al Ittihad at 31% for their rescheduled Saudi Pro League clash at Riyadh's SHG Arena, reflecting a razor-thin edge for the hosts despite Ittihad's stronger 6th-place standing (45 points from 28 games) over Shabab's 12th (31 points). Recent postponement from April 10 due to AFC Champions League commitments has kept focus on squad fitness, with Ittihad hampered by Mahamadou Doumbia's long-term cruciate tear, recent red-card suspensions for Moussa Diaby and Danilo Pereira, and minor strains like Youssef En-Nesyri's (now cleared). Shabab misses right-back Mohammed Harbush (cruciate) and winger Carlos Júnior (abdominal), but home form and Ittihad's spotty away record plus unbeaten streak snapped in recent draws neutralize historical head-to-head dominance (Ittihad won last five prior meetings), fueling the tight race.

Longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky's May 2025 retirement opened the seat, leading to a crowded March 17 primary where Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss clinched the nomination with 29% amid heavy spending exceeding $22 million. Republican nominee John Elleson, a pastor with minimal fundraising, faces steep barriers including lopsided turnout and incumbency-like Democratic advantages. While late scandals, health issues for Biss, or a massive national Republican wave could shift odds, such disruptions remain highly improbable ahead of the November 3 general election.

Jasmine Clark to 75.5% as the GA-13 Democratic primary winner on May 19, viewing the safely Democratic district's contest as an open-seat race despite Scott's name remaining on the ballot. Clark's superior fundraising, prior success flipping a Republican state House seat, and endorsements from groups like Indivisible have solidified her frontrunner status amid a crowded field. Everton Blair Jr. holds 18% on local backing, including DeKalb County DA Sherry Boston, and vows to reject corporate PAC money as crypto super PACs pour over $2 million into the race. Early voting is underway, with a special election looming for the term's remainder.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) close above $220 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Apple (AAPL) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AAPL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple's share price trades around $270, reflecting trader caution ahead of its fiscal Q2 2026 earnings release after the April 30 market close—the decisive catalyst for the end-of-April settlement. Consensus forecasts project EPS of $1.94 on anticipated iPhone and services revenue growth, bolstered by AI features, though China sales softness lingers from prior quarters. Recent leadership transition, with Tim Cook shifting to executive chairman and hardware chief John Ternus named CEO effective September, has fueled optimism, driving a rebound from mid-April $258 lows to $271 highs amid broader tech sector strength. Analyst price targets average $300, implying upside potential, yet elevated volatility and Treasury yield pressures could sway the final close.

eventMetadata.context_description: Benfica holds a slim three-point lead over Sporting CP in the Primeira Liga table after 31 matches to Sporting's 30, with 75 points to 72, fueling trader consensus of a near-coin-flip race for second place ahead of runaway leaders FC Porto. Benfica's unbeaten league campaign, capped by a dramatic 2-1 derby victory at Sporting on April 19 and a 4-1 rout of Moreirense last weekend, has bolstered their position, while Sporting's recent draw against relegated AVS allowed the gap to persist despite their superior +55 goal difference. With both facing mid-table opponents in remaining fixtures—including Sporting's game in hand against Tondela today—the outcome hinges on form and head-to-head tiebreakers.

markets.question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?
markets.description:
On October 9, 2025 Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Israel-Hamas ceasefire, effective since October 2025 under the Gaza peace plan, faces mounting strain from reciprocal violations, with the IDF reporting over a dozen Hamas breaches in mid-April and airstrikes killing at least 12 Palestinians on April 24 amid claims of 2,400 Israeli infractions since inception. Hamas rejected a full disarmament clause on April 16, stalling Phase II talks on weapons handover and post-war governance, while Israel insists on demilitarization before troop withdrawal. Regional de-escalation with Hezbollah and Iran has refocused pressure on Gaza, sustaining trader consensus for tenuous truce continuation despite escalation signals and no-confidence in diplomatic breakthroughs before summer deadlines.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 120-139 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Recent blows include Éder Militão's hamstring relapse requiring potential surgery, Rodrygo's ACL tear, and Estêvão's hamstring rupture—all within the past month—thinning defensive and winger depth under Carlo Ancelotti, preventing higher favoritism. Morocco, at 18.5%, draws value from their 2022 semifinal run and defensive resilience, though earlier AFCON injuries like Azzedine Ounahi's calf tear have healed. The 23% draw pricing underscores a tight matchup on neutral turf, with both sides prioritizing clean sheets early in group play.

markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by ≥1.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' consensus prices a modest April 2026 CPI month-over-month increase at 0.5% (32.5% implied probability) and 0.6% (27.0%), reflecting caution after March's surprise 0.9% MoM surge—the hottest since 2022—fueled by spiking energy costs from geopolitical tensions. This closely contested positioning highlights uncertainty, with Cleveland Fed nowcasting 0.44% as of April 28 amid softer oil post-Iran ceasefire but persistent shelter inflation and rising year-ahead consumer expectations to 4.7%. Diverging from economist medians near 0.2-0.4%, trader capital emphasizes volatile gas prices and sticky core pressures as key differentiators ahead of the May 12 Bureau of Labor Statistics release.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas holds a dominant edge over Toronto KOI in recent head-to-heads, sweeping their last six series 0-6 with a 18-2 map advantage, capped by a 3-1 elimination win in the CDL Minor I tournament on April 25 where Mercules and Shotzzy delivered hardpoint masterclasses on maps like Scar and Den. Both teams entered Stage 3 Major Qualifiers strongly—Toronto KOI at 2-0 (6-2 map record) after wins over Riyadh Falcons and others, OpTic Texas 1-0 (3-1)—fueled by stable rosters featuring Shotzzy, Dashy, Huke, and Mercules for Texas, and Insight, CleanX, JoeDeceives, Kips for KOI. Recent scrims showed competitiveness (e.g., 4-3, 5-5 map splits), but trader consensus reflects Texas' superior form, meta adaptation on Black Ops 6 maps like Gridlock and Exposure, and momentum heading into this BO5 qualifier bracket match.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Recent testimony in the OpenAI trial may spur short bursts, but upcoming company earnings or launches could differentiate outcomes, underscoring the unpredictability of celebrity social media rhythms backed by real-money trader wisdom.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 60% implied probability for Utah's 1st Congressional District Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his fundraising dominance—holding as much cash on hand as the rest of the field combined—name recognition from his 2019-2021 service, and a March Data for Progress poll showing 36% support among likely primary voters. Nate Blouin's 26.5% reflects progressive enthusiasm as a state senator criticizing McAdams' moderate record on issues like minimum wage and reproductive rights, amid recent attack ads and debates. At the April 26 Utah Democratic convention, McAdams captured 40% on the first ballot before losing ranked-choice endorsement to newcomer Liban Mohamed, but traders view the delegate-heavy event as left-skewed versus the broader primary electorate, positioning McAdams' base for a plurality win in the newly drawn blue-leaning district. Lower odds for Brian King, Caroline Gleich, and others highlight their limited polling and visibility.

markets.question: Will Nolan McLean win the 2026 NL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Yoshinobu Yamamoto rounds out top pricing at 11%, buoyed by Dodgers' contender status, as skin-in-the-game traders weigh innings durability, WHIP, and team win support in this wide-open race.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 275M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) stocks stood at 405 million barrels for the week ending April 17, 2026, down about 4 million barrels from the prior week amid ongoing Department of Energy (DOE) loan exchanges totaling over 40 million barrels since March. These drawdowns respond to global supply tightness from Middle East escalations, including Strait of Hormuz disruptions and sanctions, despite elevated U.S. commercial crude inventories near 460 million barrels. Trader consensus implies an 88% probability of SPR falling to 400 million barrels by June 5, with lower thresholds like 375 million at 73%, reflecting expectations of continued releases through summer driving season but limited by high domestic production and refinery utilization at 90%. Weekly EIA reports and further DOE announcements on the 172-million-barrel exchange program will drive near-term shifts.

markets.question: Spread: AS Monaco FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AS Monaco FC" if AS Monaco FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Metz".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Metz vs. AS Monaco FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 2 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Metz sit 18th in Ligue 1 with 26 points from 30 matches, mired in a relegation scrap, hosting seventh-placed AS Monaco on May 2 at Stade Saint-Symphorien amid a mounting injury crisis including Maxime Colin (calf), Boubacar Traoré (knee), and Believe Munongo out. Metz's porous defense concedes heavily, worsened by recent struggles against bottom-table rivals. Monaco, with 49 points from 29 games, endured a 4-1 loss to Paris FC last week despite Folarin Balogun extending his scoring streak to nine straight appearances and Paul Pogba's injury return, but remain potent away with an unbeaten run in eight head-to-heads versus Metz, capped by September's 5-2 win. Monaco's long-term absentees like Takumi Minamino (knee) and Mohammed Salisu (cruciate) test depth, yet their form and matchup edge shape trader consensus toward visiting superiority in this table-climbing fixture.

markets.question: Will Chad Bianco win the California Governor Election in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to according to the candidate who wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026.

If the results of the election are not confirmed by July 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: With California's nonpartisan top-two primary for governor set for June 2, trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Democrat Tom Steyer at 47% implied probability for outright victory, driven by his unmatched fundraising dominance—outspending rivals through self-funding as a billionaire climate activist—which bolsters his polling ties near the top (14-17% in recent Emerson and Gudelunas surveys) and path to consolidate Democratic support in the blue state general election. Former HHS Secretary and Attorney General Xavier Becerra trails at 30%, surging post-debate to 15-21% in Emerson, EMC, and Gudelunas polls due to his extensive government experience appealing to voters prioritizing affordability and leadership amid economic concerns topping 41% of respondents. Republicans Steve Hilton (8%) and Sheriff Chad Bianco lead some primary polls at 17-20%, but markets discount GOP general odds given California's Democratic lean and historical top-two dynamics favoring the party in November. Recent exits by frontrunner Eric Swalwell and Betty Yee have redistributed Democratic votes, tightening the field.

VP JD Vance leads Republican nominee odds at 19% despite foreign policy setbacks such as failed Iran negotiations, while Gavin Newsom at 18% bolsters Democratic prospects via strong hypothetical matchups and opposition to Trump administration policies; upcoming 2026 midterms and swing state polling remain pivotal catalysts.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no comprehensive AI safety bill passing before 2027, reflecting stalled federal legislative progress amid the Trump administration's light-touch approach. The White House's March 20 National Policy Framework urged Congress for industry-friendly rules, federal preemption of state AI laws, and narrow protections like child safety and deepfakes, but no broad safety mandate advanced. Q1 2026 saw dozens of targeted bills introduced—such as Sen. Blackburn's TRUMP AMERICA AI Act draft and measures on nonconsensual images—yet none cleared committees for floor votes in the divided 119th Congress. States like California and Colorado enacted their own regulations, filling the void as bipartisan momentum focuses on specifics rather than sweeping safety reforms ahead of 2026 midterms.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).
